title: Droid Statutes
content:
{{Top|leg}}
'''Droid Statutes''' were specific punitive measures enacted against owners of lawbreaking [[Droid/Legends|droids]] by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], in the wake of the [[Great Droid Revolution]] of [[4015 BBY]].<ref name="NEGTD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> They were eventually codified by [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|COMPNOR]] in the [[Imperial Penal References]]. There were five classes of infractions, ranking from Class Five to Class One in severity.<ref name="FTD">''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]''</ref>

Typically, the owner of a droid would be held responsible for the infraction and punished accordingly. If the owner of the droid was convicted of the crime, the droid was usually [[Memory wipe/Legends|memory-wiped]] and then auctioned to offset the costs of the trial. In extreme cases, the droid would be confiscated and destroyed.<ref name="FTD" />

==Classes==
===Class Five infractions===
'''Class Five infractions''' were the lowest misdemeanor crimes. They were punishable by a small fine of between 100 and 1,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for the owner and a memory wipe for the droid. They were often overlooked by local [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement]].<ref name="FTD" />
Several examples of Class Five infractions were:
*theft in amounts up to 5,000 credits<ref name="FTD" />
*verbal assault on an organic being<ref name="FTD" />
*lack of a [[Restraining bolt/Legends|restraining bolt]] or other "leashing" technology<ref name="FTD" />

===Class Four infractions===
'''Class Four infractions''' were normally the most violent droid-related crimes. They were punishable by a 500 to 3,000 credit fine for the owner and a memory wipe for the droid.<ref name="FTD" /> Several examples of Class Four infractions were:
*Theft in amounts between 5,000 and 10,000 credits<ref name="FTD" />
*Possession or installation of restricted programming without a permit<ref name="FTD" />
*Property damage, including other droids<ref name="FTD" />
*Trespassing on droid-restricted property<ref name="FTD" />

===Class Three infractions===
'''Class Three infractions''' were usually overlooked by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] personnel and delegated to local authorities. They were punishable by a fine between 1,000 and 5,000 credits for the owner and a mandatory memory wipe and reset on the droid. In some cases, the owner was found criminally liable and the droid was impounded for public auction.<ref name="FTD" />
Several examples of Class Three infractions were:
*Theft in amounts exceeding 10,000 credits<ref name="FTD" />
*Ownership or installation of a weapon without a permit<ref name="FTD" />
*Physical assault not concluding with [[Death/Legends|death]]<ref name="FTD" />

===Class Two infractions===
'''Class Two infractions''' were rare and usually highly publicized. They were punishable by confiscation of the droid and imprisonment of the owner for between five and thirty [[Galactic Standard Calendar|standard year]] in a high security prison like [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. In extreme cases, the confiscated droid was destroyed, but most often it was simply memory wiped and sold in a public auction.<ref name="FTD" />
Several examples of Class Two infractions were:
*Involuntary manslaughter<ref name="FTD" />
*Ownership or installation of an illegal weapon with the droid's chassis<ref name="FTD" />
*Illegal programming<ref name="FTD" />
*Unauthorized espionage programming<ref name="FTD" />

===Class One infractions===
'''Class One infractions''' were illegalities that were considered capital offenses. They were often covered up by authorities so that they wouldn't panic the general population. They were punishable by imprisonment of the owner for five years to life and destruction of the droid.<ref name="FTD" />
Several examples of Class One infractions were:
*Conspiring to overthrow the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="FTD" />
*Voluntary manslaughter<ref name="FTD" />
*Programming or actions with intent of harming Imperial personnel<ref name="FTD" />
*Programming a droid to engage in espionage activities against the Empire or its citizens<ref name="FTD" />

==Enforcement==
Enforcement of the Droid Statutes varied widely. Some technologically-advanced [[Planet/Legends|planets]] like [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and [[Derilyn]] lightened punishments for less significant infractions of the Statutes so as not to tie up local [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement]] unnecessarily, while remote worlds like [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and [[Gamorr/Legends|Gamorr]] had their judiciaries bogged down for months by droid-committed crime. Some [[Planetary Security Forces|planetary militias]] created special [[Droid enforcement agent|droid enforcement agencies]] to specialize in investigating and prosecuting violations of the Droid Statutes.<ref name="FTD" />

==Sources==
*<!-- 1997-04-XX -->''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]'' {{1stm}}
*<!-- 2006-06-27 -->''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''
*<!-- 2008-12-09 -->''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''
